text:
In the quiet suburb of Maplewood stood an old, redbrick elementary school. The school's mascot was the teddy bear, Miss Adelaide. She was the life of the school, festooned with sequins and patches. It was spring, and the gardens were flourishing. Children were playing tag outdoors, while others arranged a school-wide hopitalite.

It was a Saturday morning, and the usual crowd had gathered at the old gymnasium. Mr. Jenkins was giving his weekly report to the parents club. Mrs. Thompson, a cheery woman in her fifties with twinkling eyes, bounced up and down. Across from her sat Mr. Harrison, whose spectacles perched on the bridge of his nose. He fidgeted with a piece of chalk, his hands pale in contrast to the dark timber of the room.

Then, the bell chimed.

"Class, quiet down!" Jenkins announced. "Today is all about ecology and action. We're planting trees!"
